__fixture__.name: moved_file
__fixture__.scenario: User moves src/lib/foo.rs to src/foo.rs. On a POSIX root the canonical object is unchanged (same inode); canonical_uri and presentation_path.uri update. The alias_set does not accumulate a stale alias; the VFS prunes the old path on rename commit. A type-index producer keyed only on the logical URI reports partial with reason partial_enumeration until its next sweep; producers keyed on the strongest identity token remain exact.

semantic_readiness.explainer: The Rust type index is redriving its logical-URI mapping after the rename. Symbol search narrows to lexical matches until the next sweep completes.

not_ready_explainer.title: Symbol coverage is catching up
not_ready_explainer.body: src/foo.rs was renamed from src/lib/foo.rs. The Rust type index keyed the prior logical URI; its next sweep will restore exact coverage. The canonical file on disk is unchanged and fully editable.
what_still_works: Lexical search (results may show both old and new spellings until the index refreshes)
Git history, blame, and diff against the moved file

safe_next_actions.consequence: No action required; the next index sweep completes automatically.
Triggers an immediate index sweep for the affected roots.
